title: US Broker-Dealer Relationship Disclosure
content:
MERIDIAN WEALTH PARTNERS - US BROKER-DEALER RELATIONSHIP DISCLOSURE

Effective Date: March 15, 2025

This disclosure document provides essential information regarding Meridian Wealth Partners' broker-dealer services and our regulatory obligations under SEC Regulation Best Interest (Regulation BI) and FINRA Rule 2267. All associates must understand these requirements to maintain compliance with federal securities laws.

BROKER-DEALER CAPACITY AND FIDUCIARY OBLIGATIONS

Meridian Wealth Partners operates as a registered broker-dealer under SEC oversight and maintains FINRA membership (CRD# 158247). When acting in our broker-dealer capacity, we are subject to Regulation BI's four key obligations: disclosure, care, conflict of interest, and compliance. Unlike investment advisers, broker-dealers are not held to a continuous fiduciary standard but must meet the "best interest" standard at the time of recommendation.

Our broker-dealer services include securities transactions, underwriting participations, and distribution of mutual funds, exchange-traded funds, and structured products. Transaction-based compensation ranges from $25-$150 per equity trade, with bond markups typically between 0.25%-1.5% depending on maturity and credit quality. Municipal bond spreads average 0.75% for investment-grade securities.

COMPENSATION AND CONFLICTS OF INTEREST

FINRA Rule 2267 requires disclosure of material conflicts that may influence recommendations. Meridian receives various forms of compensation that create potential conflicts:

• Mutual fund 12b-1 fees ranging from 0.25%-1.00% annually
• Revenue sharing from preferred fund families averaging $2.3 million quarterly
• Underwriting compensation of 3-7% on new issue corporate bonds
• Insurance product commissions between 1-5% of premium values
• Third-party research payments totaling approximately $450,000 annually

These arrangements are disclosed in our Form CRS and individual transaction confirmations as required by Regulation BI's disclosure obligation.

SUITABILITY AND BEST INTEREST DETERMINATIONS

Prior to making recommendations, associates must conduct reasonable diligence regarding the customer's investment profile, including risk tolerance, time horizon, liquidity needs, and financial situation. Our enhanced suitability process requires documentation of:

• Customer investment objectives and constraints
• Quantitative suitability analysis for recommended portfolio allocations
• Reasonable basis suitability review of complex products
• Customer-specific suitability assessment for each recommendation

For high-risk investments such as non-traded REITs, structured notes, or alternative investments, additional supervisory approval is required for accounts below $500,000 in liquid net worth.

REGULATORY REPORTING AND DOCUMENTATION

Compliance with Regulation BI requires comprehensive documentation of the recommendation process. Associates must maintain records demonstrating:

• Reasonable basis for believing recommendations are in the customer's best interest
• Analysis of costs, risks, and potential benefits
• Consideration of reasonably available alternatives
• Documentation supporting conflict mitigation efforts

FINRA Rule 2267's enhanced supervision requirements mandate quarterly reviews of customer account activity, with particular attention to concentration risk, excessive trading, and unsuitable product recommendations.

This disclosure must be provided to customers before or at account opening and updated annually or when material changes occur. Associates should direct regulatory questions to the Compliance Department at compliance@meridianwealth.com or extension 4200.

By acknowledging this disclosure, you confirm understanding of our broker-dealer relationship structure, compensation arrangements, and regulatory obligations under current securities laws.
